title: The Story of When Samantha Began
author: Dady Fredy
subtitle: A Bedtime Story by Dady Fredy
dedication: For Samantha — we loved you before we knew you.

lines: Tap, tap, tap…
small heart, brand new.
Long before the world began,
we already loved you.

pages.title: The Story of When Samantha Began
pages.subtitle: A Bedtime Story by Dady Fredy
pages.dedication: For Samantha — we loved you before we knew you.

lines: One quiet night, tucked into bed,
Samantha looked at Dady and said,
"Dady Fredy… tell me, please—
Where was I before these trees?"
He smiled and sat beside the light,
And softly started the story of the night:
"Before your laugh, before your name,
Before your tiny footsteps came—"
"You lived with Mama, warm and small,
No bigger than a raindrop's fall.
Smaller than a plum tree seed,
Smaller than a word, a wish, a bead."
"Mama's heart became a room,
A place where you could safely bloom.
And every day you grew a bit—
Tiny hands that curled to fists,
Tiny feet like little twists,
A little wiggle, soft and quick."
"She'd feel you stretch, she'd feel you turn,
Each new day something new you'd learn.
And Mama laughed and rubbed her belly,
Feeling you — all soft and jelly."
"And Dady Fredy? He talked to you, too—
Even though you couldn't see,
He'd lean in close and whisper free:
'We can't wait to meet you someday,
We already love you in every way.'"
"Your heart began to beat so small—
Tap… tap… tap — the sweetest sound of all.
Your ears learned voices, warm and near:
Mama's voice… and Dady, dear."
Then one special, magical day,
You were finally ready to come and play.
Mama was brave, the doctors were kind,
And Dady Fredy paced with a racing mind—
Until a small cry filled the air,
And there you were — beyond compare.
So tiny, new, with eyes so blue,
Ten little fingers — all brand new.
Dady Fredy held you near—
Too full of joy to shed a tear.
And Mama kissed your little head
And whispered, "Welcome, love," she said.
And from that moment — warm and true —
The world grew brighter because of you.
The stars leaned in, the moon stood still,
And everything whispered: Samantha will.
Samantha murmured, sleepy and deep,
Already drifting close to sleep:
"I was loved there… before I knew?"
"Yes, anak… long, long before you grew.
Mama carried you with love and care,
And Dady Fredy? I was always there."
So close your eyes now, my Samantha, my dear,
The story of you is resting right here.
Before your first breath, before your first cry—
You were already the light in your Mama's and Dady's eye.
Goodnight, Samantha.
Goodnight, anak.
